what_it_does: Reads design/register.json and design/_system/addresses.json and deterministically emits generated blueprint artifacts: design/blueprint/component-inventory.json (region/address-to-component mapping with built/planned status) and per-view surface specs under design/blueprint/surfaces/<home-journey>/<view-id>.json, using journey membership, a lowest-numbered home-journey rule, and cross-journey edge refs.
observations: Generated outputs are explicitly marked as never hand-edited; the script wipes and rebuilds design/blueprint/surfaces/.
Maps UI regions to React component files via hardcoded REGION_COMPONENT, distinguishing built vs planned status to avoid fiction.
Computes view-to-journey membership from sequence steps and assigns each view a deterministic home_journey (lowest journey id) plus cross_journeys for graph edges the directory tree cannot hold.
Joins views to registered ui:// addresses by feature-id (represents); flags address gaps instead of inventing addresses.
Includes a shared component list and per-view build_to hints (components, design_lint, tokens).
Heavy use of docstring comments to encode design law and source-of-truth contracts.
standouts: The built/planned honesty mechanism: generated rows only claim a component path when it is asserted to exist elsewhere.
Deterministic graph reconstruction via home_journey directory spine + cross_journeys address refs (B3).
Address-gap surfacing rather than fabrication, enforcing the no-invented-addresses law.
conventions: Treats docstrings and module comments as authoritative design contracts (LAW, SOURCE OF TRUTH, GENERATES).
Uses deterministic sorting (sorted with jnum) so output is reproducible.
Hardcodes domain mappings (REGION_COMPONENT, SHARED_COMPONENTS) inside the emitter rather than reading them from register/addresses.
Opens files inline without with-statements in load() and build_component_inventory(), relying on GC closure.
Uses shutil.rmtree to clean the generated directory before rewriting.
No CLI argument parsing; paths are resolved relative to __file__.
concerns: Hardcoded REGION_COMPONENT and SHARED_COMPONENTS can drift from the actual canvas/app/src file tree and must be maintained manually.
load() and build_component_inventory() open files without explicit close, which is a minor resource-leak smell.
addresses_for_view() rebuilds the feature-to-address index on every call instead of caching it once per emit.
No schema validation of register.json or addresses.json before processing.
Wiping surfaces/ without backup could lose any unexpected manually placed files.
Relies on external acceptance tests (blueprint_acceptance.py, check.py) to enforce existence and lint; the emitter itself does not verify component files exist.
notes: A deterministic blueprint generator that turns the design register and address registry into per-view surface specs and a component inventory, surfacing gaps rather than inventing data.
questions: What is the exact JSON schema of design/register.json (views, sequences, areas) and design/_system/addresses.json?
How does navgraph.py read the generated surfaces directory back, and does it rely on any fields not shown here?
What do F0, F8, and B6 refer to in the region notes, and how do they gate status?
What checks does blueprint_acceptance.py perform on built component paths?
How are tokens.json and design-system.css consumed by the builder?
What happens when a view appears in multiple sequences with the same lowest journey id but different ordering?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file loads the stated source-of-truth files and deterministically emits the described blueprint artifacts, including component inventory and surface specs with journey/cross-journey edges.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to enforce a machine-readable design system where every view, journey edge, UI address, and rendering component is linked through deterministic generation, with explicit gaps surfaced for human resolution rather than silently patched.
novelty: The home-journey directory spine combined with cross_journeys address-refs is a distinct way to serialize a graph (tree + web) into the filesystem so it can be read back by navgraph.py.
contribution: Contributes a deterministic, repeatable emitter that translates design-register and address-registry facts into per-view surface specifications and a component inventory, preserving journey graph edges and distinguishing built from planned components without fabricating addresses.
summary_for_embedding: A Python emitter that reads design/register.json and design/_system/addresses.json to deterministically generate a component inventory and per-view surface specs organized by home journey, with cross-journey refs and address-gap detection.
intention_for_embedding: To automate the creation of an honest, reconstructible UI blueprint from canonical design data while preventing invented addresses and hiding unbuilt surfaces.
suggested_fields: generation_inputs: explicit list of source files and keys consumed.
artifact_outputs: paths and schemas of generated files.
status_map: built/planned mapping and its external validation gate.
generated_file_policy: wipe/rebuild rule and hand-edit prohibition.

symbols.does: Loads register.json and addresses.json into memory.
symbols.description: Opens the two source-of-truth JSON files relative to the script location and returns their parsed contents; it performs no schema validation.

symbols.does: Converts a journey id like J7 into a sortable integer.
symbols.description: Strips the leading 'J' and returns the numeric portion so journey ids can be ordered deterministically.

symbols.does: Builds the set of journeys whose sequence steps contain each view.
symbols.description: Iterates every sequence, collects which views appear in its steps, and returns a mapping from view id to a sorted list of journey ids; empty list means an unrouted view.

symbols.does: Selects the lowest-numbered journey containing a view as its tree home.
symbols.description: Returns the first journey id from the view's membership list, or '_unrouted' when the view appears in no journey; this drives the output directory spine.

symbols.does: Joins a view to its registered ui:// addresses by feature id.
symbols.description: Looks up addresses whose represents field matches one of the view's represents values, deduplicates and sorts them, and returns a gap note when the view claims features but no address joins.
Groups registered addresses by region, maps each region to a component path and built/planned status, and appends the shared component list to produce component-inventory.json.

symbols.does: Constructs a single per-view surface specification.
symbols.description: Combines a view's metadata, journey membership, home journey, cross-journey refs, joined addresses, and build-to guidance into the JSON object written to the surfaces tree.

symbols.does: Orchestrates wiping and rewriting the blueprint outputs.
symbols.description: Loads sources, computes journey membership, removes and recreates the surfaces directory, writes every view's surface spec, writes the component inventory, and prints a summary plus any address gaps.
